strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput data systems — meets the 15-year floor on paper.
Polyglot builder with genuine breadth (TypeScript, DLang, C/C++, Java, Node.js) and some hands-on agentic/MCP tooling at re:cinq since April 2025.
Community teaching (workshops, conference talk, open-source GISCollective) shows willingness to share knowledge externally.
concerns: AI expertise is a thin 3-month entry on the resume (re:cinq, April 2025); no prior AI/ML work, no demonstrated AI product or platform at scale, and no academic grounding in the field — directly contradicting the 'recognized AI expertise' requirement.

Career trajectory is Senior/Staff Software Developer (executor), not Director-level IC; there is zero evidence of advising strategic customers, setting technical direction across an organization, or influencing at the level the role demands.

Cloud experience is shallow — one peripheral AWS CDK mention at Optilyz; no cloud architecture, no cloud platform ownership, and no engagement with the strategic cloud-plus-AI positioning the role centers on.
response.justification: This is a hands-on Senior/Staff engineer who is skilled at building things and recently began touching AI tooling, but the role is a Director-level principal charged with shaping AI strategy, advising enterprise customers, and demonstrating recognized expertise in AI — none of which this resume evidences; the candidate's entire AI exposure post-dates the job posting by roughly three months, their cloud engagement is incidental rather than architectural, and there is no signal of the external thought-leadership or organizational influence the position requires, making them a poor fit for the scope and seniority of this role.
